front: How does Iceberg support row-level deletes and updates — MERGE INTO and DELETE FROM?
back: Iceberg supports row-level DML operations that are not possible on plain Parquet files on S3. DELETE FROM removes specific rows; UPDATE changes column values; MERGE INTO is the upsert operation (insert new rows, update existing ones by key). Since Parquet files are immutable, Iceberg implements row-level changes using one of two strategies: Copy-on-Write (CoW) or Merge-on-Read (MoR). In CoW mode, DELETE rewrites the entire affected data file without the deleted rows — expensive at write time but fast reads. In MoR mode, DELETE writes a small delete file (either positional — listing file+row positions — or equality — listing key values) without touching the original data file. At read time, the engine merges data files with delete files on the fly. MERGE INTO is the most powerful for CDC ingestion: `MERGE INTO target t USING source s ON t.id = s.id WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET * WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT *;` This is table stakes for streaming lakehouse pipelines.
